page: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmsv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8573C processor with a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 6 dedicated vCPUs derived from 3 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 252.0 GB, translating to 42.0 GB of RAM per core, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core performance. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2350.0 and a text processing score of 2902.0, both placing in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression at 34.92 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.31 GB/sec also rank in the top 25%. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall into the average range, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 6664.64 and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 50.68 GB/sec. However, resource-intensive tasks such as medium and large model LLM inference show weaker performance, with text generation on a 7B model yielding 6.65 tokens/sec and prompt processing on a 70B model at 6.03 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX12-6ms_v2 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades off local storage and GPU acceleration for massive memory density per vCPU. This resource distribution is highly optimized for workloads that require large in-memory datasets but do not require massive parallel multi-core processing. It is an appropriate fit for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing applications.
description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides 6 vCPUs across 3 physical cores and an extensive 252.0 GB of RAM, yielding 42.0 GB of memory per core. It features no local storage or GPU resources but is premium storage capable. Benchmarks highlight exceptional single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reaching the top 10% tier, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core metrics and large-model LLM inference speeds remain average to weak. This configuration represents a highly specialized, memory-dense profile designed for workloads that prioritize RAM capacity over raw core counts. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 6 vCPUs, 252 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6ms_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 6 vCPUs, 252 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for in-memory databases.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense virtual server optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and large in-memory database applications.
bullet_points: Powered by a high-frequency 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 6 dedicated vCPUs.
Features an exceptional 252.0 GB of RAM, providing 42.0 GB of memory per core.
Top-tier single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core and text processing.
No local storage or GPU resources included, but supports premium external storage.
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
